questions.explanation: Forests maintain oxygen-CO₂ balance, water cycle, soil fertility, and provide habitat for wildlife.
Without trees, soil becomes loose and gets washed away by rain or blown away by wind, causing erosion.

questions.correctAnswer: Organisms that break down dead plants and animals into humus.
questions.explanation: Decomposers like bacteria and fungi break down dead matter, recycling nutrients back to soil.

questions.text: The branched part of a tree above the stem is called ________.
questions.explanation: The crown is the branched part of a tree above the stem. It contains leaves for photosynthesis.
